author: John Carmack
message:
Execute window actions when the window id is unavailable (#1788)

* Execute window actions when the window id is unavailable

After a session transition (waking to the login window, or an
idle-induced logout), macOS can stop vending window ids and window
lists to accessibility clients until the screen is locked and unlocked
again (#640, #1244, #1325, #1673). Rectangle treated the window id as
required for every action, so keyboard, menu, and URL actions beeped
and did nothing, and drag to snap went dead, even though the per-app
accessibility connection still worked.

The window id is only used for history bookkeeping (restore rects,
repeated-action cycling, todo and stage checks). Make it optional:
actions now execute without it and skip only the id-keyed bookkeeping.
Restore still requires an id since it is entirely history-driven.

Also add a last-resort window-under-cursor fallback that hit-tests the
frontmost app's own accessibility windows, since both existing lookups
(the window list and the system-wide hit test) depend on window server
info that is unavailable in this state.

* Derive a fallback window id from accessibility element identity

When both real sources fail (the broken session state in #640), derive
a bookkeeping-only id from CFHash of the window's AXUIElement, which is
stable for the same window across fetches and unaffected by moves or
resizes. The high bit keeps derived ids out of the real window id
space. Restores cycling, restore rects, and unsnap history in the
broken state.

* Fix nil errors from merge from main

---------

Co-authored-by: Ryan Hanson <ryan@ryanhanson.dev>

author: epicdistraction
message:
Cooperative Window Resizer Feature (#1780)

* Add cooperative window planner to corner actions

* Cooperative cycles on side actions.

* Allow the cooperative window manager to resize both corner and side actions

* Fix issue where a corner window doesn't resize when hidden by side window

* Fix issue where <50% side split ratios would invoke a cyclic action at the time of moving window to a different corner.

* Update Main.xcstrings to upstream/main and add branch changes

* refactor cooperative resize behavior so it is constraint-aware and applies the entire resize plan once.

* Logic refactor WIP gate. Respects min size on refresh. wider tolerance for smaller apps. Allows for gap resize feature

* Wider tolerances for nearby matches for windows not exactly grid size.
Min width will force the split ratio to be moved to the nearest successful split.
Logic to picking up nearby windows that are cutoff by the side split and wider range capture.
Incorporates gap between windows feature.

* Fix outer perimeter to prevent window creep into gap area

* If enabled, use cooperative resizer in dragggable snap areas.

* Add a window target resolver to avoid pushing to default size and capturing the opposite windows.

* Refactor Cooperative Resizer functionality in Window Manager into an extension

* Refine cooperative corner resize preflight and cleanup
Use realized constrained/cyclic corner boundaries for initial placement, avoid cleanup from reduced adjacent corners, and make repeated cycling recognize cooperative gap-adjusted frames.

* Dynamic Side Split Ratio

* Revert "Dynamic Side Split Ratio"

This reverts commit 299a73f2d229f96f1142f1e8761d35f9b06294a6.

* Fix cleanup resize for focused adjacent corner

* Reapply "Dynamic Side Split Ratio"

This reverts commit 315f0f03e3d2201dd896e71343d8021ad1217dc5.

* * WindowCalculationResult.initialRect can now be updated after calculation.
* Cooperative corner resizing now skips a cycle step when an adjacent window is already min-height restricted.
* Side-split ratio recording now uses the intended raw cycle size when a cycle step is skipped.
* Cleanup logic is stricter so valid cycled corners are not resized by mistake.
* Cleanup can still shrink a corner when that old source corner is stuck between cycle sizes because of a min-height restriction.
* Added tests for the new cycling and cleanup edge cases.

* * resize cleaup on side-by side windows
* Fix logging history of cooperative actions
* Update window state when neighbor resizes it to prevent duplicate actions/cyclic actions out of order.

* Handle resizing adjacent min-size restricted window when min-size window moves away and there is nothing behind it.

* * Update runtime split ratios from the final cooperative layout after macOS applies window minimum-size constraints. Preserve gap-aware boundaries without changing saved preferences.
* Also refine cycle handling so windows opposite a minimum-height-restricted window can wrap past an impossible maximum size, and resize remaining corner windows toward the next smaller cycle size when a restricted corner is vacated.
* Add regression coverage for constrained widths/heights, symmetric corners, gaps, cyclic continuation, preference preservation, blocked maximum cycling, and corner cleanup.

---------

Co-authored-by: Daniel <daniel@trainoperator.co>

author: epicdistraction

Apply Half-Split ratio to corner actions and snap areas (#1776)

* Half size updates

* Use half Ratio sizes in Corner Calculations

* Add lock horizontal/vertical axis feature. Fix cyclic hotkeys hitting repeating the same state. Fix moving window flash after resize.

* Prefer user-centric label over implementation.

* A little more spacing for cycle sizes settings

author: Gunnar Von Bergen
message:
Adds a "Skip gap on top screen edge" checkbox in Settings (#1773)

* Adds a "Skip gap on top screen edge" checkbox in Settings (near the gap size slider).
When enabled, windows positioned at the top of the screen will sit flush against the menu bar area rather than having the gap applied there. Gaps between windows and on all other screen edges are unaffected. Default is off, preserving existing behavior.

* Rename "Skip gap" to "Remove gap" on top screen edge option label

author: dooyeoung.ryu
message:
feat: add Combined Display Mode (#1763)

* feat: add Combined Display Mode

Add a setting to treat multiple monitors as a single virtual display,
so window actions (half, center, maximize, corners, etc.) operate across
all connected monitors at once.

Settings window: "Treat multiple monitors as one" checkbox with description,
separated by a divider line from other options. Localized via NSLocalizedString
with Korean translation in Main.xcstrings.

When enabled:
- All window calculations receive a combined bounding frame of all monitors
- BestEffortWindowMover uses the combined frame to avoid single-screen clamping
- Left/Right half, Center, CenterProminently respect the combined frame
- Next/Previous Display menu items are hidden

Implementation:
- Defaults.combinedDisplayMode (BoolDefault) persists the setting
- WindowCalculationParameters carries combinedDisplayFrame: CGRect?
- WindowManager computes the union of all screen visible frames when enabled

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: move combined display logic into adjustedVisibleFrame

Per maintainer feedback, compute the union of all screens inside
adjustedVisibleFrame so every calculation picks it up automatically
without threading a combinedDisplayFrame parameter through
WindowCalculationParameters and each individual calculation file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: early return in combined display mode to skip per-screen adjustments

When combined display mode is active, return the union immediately so
stage strip, todo sidebar, and screen edge gap adjustments (all derived
from self, a single NSScreen) are not incorrectly applied to the
multi-screen combined frame.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: move combined display logic into adjustedVisibleFrame

Per maintainer feedback, compute the union of all screens inside
adjustedVisibleFrame so every calculation picks it up automatically.
Early return after the union to avoid incorrectly applying per-screen
adjustments (stage strip, todo sidebar, edge gaps) to the combined frame.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove whitespace changes

* Switch to optional bool default & move checkbox logic into function

* Accommodate todo mode

---------

author: Jakub Anderwald
message:
Delete SECURITY_AUDIT.md (#1767)

This was a one-off report to elaborate on a security fix PR I pushed. No need to keep it permanently in the repo.

author: Jakub Anderwald
message:
security: pin MASShortcut, confirm URL tasks, harden config auto-load (#1765)

Addresses three issues identified in a source-level security audit
(see SECURITY_AUDIT.md for the full report):

1. [High] MASShortcut SPM dependency was pinned to `branch = master`,
   meaning every build pulled the latest tip with no review gate. Pin
   to the current HEAD revision SHA so future upstream changes require
   an explicit bump.

2. [Medium] `rectangle://execute-task=ignore-app/unignore-app` URL
   actions silently mutated the disabled-apps defaults when triggered
   from outside Rectangle (e.g. a web page). Require an NSAlert
   confirmation, surfacing the bundle-id, unless Rectangle itself is
   frontmost.

3. [Medium] `Config.loadFromSupportDir()` silently auto-loaded any
   RectangleConfig.json dropped in Application Support on launch. Add
   a confirmation prompt, reject symlinks and world-writable files,
   and apply a 1 MiB size cap to the loader (defense against OOM via
   giant config).

Also includes SECURITY_AUDIT.md documenting the full review, including
confirmation that the app contains no telemetry, analytics, or crash
reporting and that the only outbound network channel is the
EdDSA-signed Sparkle update feed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

author: Myron Koch
message:
Fix false positive overlap detection for half/third actions (#1766)

Origin-only matching caused false positives when a larger window
(e.g. maximized or near-fullscreen) shared the same origin as the
target position. Now excludes windows that cover more than 90% of
the screen on both axes from overlap detection, while preserving
offset behavior for mixed-size grid windows at the same position
(e.g. an eighth behind a quarter).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
